notes: SHIP v1.14.241 — B300 LIQUID ORV3 TRAY = 2U (data-only, one MASTER_U_TABLE row; John ruling 2026-07-13, vendor spec: Supermicro SYS-222GS-NB3OT-ALC class, 2-OU HGX B300 liquid tray). Seeds the SPARKS (US-SPK03) fleet. ⚠️ DFW02 IS COMPLETELY UNAFFECTED — the string b300 does not occur in the DFW2 master, so every DFW02 host resolves to exactly the same height as .240 and the in-flight .238/.239/.240 device pass on DFW2 REMAINS VALID. The only visible difference on a DFW2 device is the build badge reading v1.14.241. SANITY-CHECK (John rule: pitch and chassis must agree, same test that caught DFW02): SPARKS gpu-b300-01 sits at a UNIFORM 3U pitch — 1152 pairs, zero exceptions, 9 nodes per rack at U11,14,17,20,23,26,29,32,35. A 2U tray + 1U service gap fits exactly. An 8U air-cooled chassis is IMPOSSIBLE there: seeding 8U would have flagged 1872/4143 devices (45%) in collision — a second 61%-red disaster on the other real site. The rack itself confirms liquid cooling: a cdu-4ru-03 (COOLANT DISTRIBUTION UNIT) sits at U2, which is the ORV3 branch, not air-cooled 19in EIA. Verified after seeding: SPARKS collisions 0/4143; resolution 37.5% -> 68.8%. GUARD-TESTED — the new row is keyed / b300 / and the word boundary means gb300 CANNOT reach it: gb300-nvl72 still resolves 1U (NVL72 tray), gb200/b200 still 1U, NVIDIA HGX H200 8-GPU still 6U (DFW02), H100-HGX still 8U (TST99), nvlink still excluded, in-name NRU still wins (ps-1ru-06 -> 1U, cdu-4ru-03 -> 4U). STILL UNKNOWN ON SPARKS (flagged gold, never guessed): q3400-ra, sn5610, sn4700, cpu-gp2-*, inf-med-01, cm8148, om2216-c14, patch panels, ngfw-4245, sn3420, net-ufm-05 — 31.2% of hosts. These produce ZERO collisions (1U placeholder + spacious pitch), so SPARKS renders honest gold, never falsely red. NOTE: DFW02 and SPARKS use INCOMPATIBLE model vocabularies (vendor marketing names vs NetBox slugs) — the strongest argument yet for promoting the SITE PROFILE work; a single fleet-wide model->U table cannot serve both.
